info.title: lofi
info.description: A CLI tool for generating and playing lofi beats with real-time effects.

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription:
A command-line application for generating and playing lofi beats

Examples:
  Start the service:            lofi start
  Generate a jazzy beat:        lofi generate --style=jazzy
  Generate with custom BPM:     lofi generate --style=chillhop --bpm=85
  Play with a specific style:   lofi play --style=chillhop
  Play with custom BPM:         lofi play --style=basic --bpm=75
  Register a sample:            lofi sample register kick /path/to/kick.wav
  Register with velocity:       lofi sample register kick-soft /path/to/kick.wav --velocity 64
  Unregister a sample:          lofi sample unregister kick
  List registered samples:      lofi sample list
  Enable vinyl effect:          lofi effect vinyl
  Adjust volume:                lofi volume --level=0.8
  Interactive mode:             lofi interactive
  Update to latest version:     lofi update
  Shutdown the service:         lofi shutdown

For more information about a command, run: lofi help <command>

commands.name: sample
commands.description: Manage user samples

commands.name: register
commands.description: Register a new sample

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all registered samples

commands.name: unregister
commands.description: Unregister a sample

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the sample to unregister

commands.name: update
commands.description:
Updates the LofiBeats CLI tool to the latest version.

Since this is distributed as a .NET tool, you can update it using:
  dotnet tool update --global LofiBeats.Cli

This will fetch and install the latest version from NuGet.

commands.name: preset
commands.description:
Save and load preset configurations including style, volume, and effects.

Subcommands:
  save <file>    Save current settings to a preset file
  load <file>    Load settings from a preset file

Examples:
  preset save mypreset.json     Save current settings
  preset load mypreset.json     Load saved settings
  preset save presets/jazz.json Save to a subdirectory

commands.name: save
commands.description: Save the current playback settings to a preset file

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: Path to the preset file (e.g., mypreset.json)

commands.name: load
commands.description: Load preset settings from a file

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: Path to the preset file (e.g., mypreset.json)

commands.name: generate
commands.description:
Creates a new beat pattern using the specified style. Each style has unique characteristics:
  - basic: Standard lofi beat pattern
  - jazzy: Syncopated rhythms with jazz-inspired elements
  - chillhop: Laid-back beats with hip-hop influence
  - hiphop: Classic hip-hop inspired beats with boom-bap elements

commands.name: play
commands.description:
Plays a new lofi beat with the specified style.

Options:
  --style           Beat style (basic, jazzy, chillhop, hiphop)
  --bpm             Tempo in beats per minute (BPM)
  --after           Specify a delay (e.g. '10m' or '30s') before starting
  --transition      Transition type: immediate or crossfade
  --xfade-duration  Crossfade duration in seconds

Examples:
  play                     Play with default style
  play --style=chillhop    Play chillhop style
  play --style=jazzy --bpm=85  Play jazzy style at 85 BPM
  play --after 5m          Start playing in 5 minutes
  play --style=hiphop --after 30s  Play hiphop style in 30 seconds
  play --transition=crossfade --xfade-duration=3  Crossfade to new pattern in 3 seconds

commands.name: stop
commands.description:
Stops the currently playing audio.

Options:
  --tapestop    Gradually slow down the audio like a tape machine powering off
  --after       Specify a delay (e.g. '10m' or '30s') before stopping

Examples:
  stop              Stop playback immediately
  stop --tapestop   Stop with tape slow-down effect
  stop --after 10m  Stop after 10 minutes
  stop --after 30s --tapestop  Stop with tape effect after 30 seconds

commands.name: pause
commands.description: Pauses audio playback

commands.name: resume
commands.description: Resumes audio playback

commands.name: effect
commands.description:
Enable or disable audio effects to enhance your lofi beats.

Available effects:
  - vinyl:       Adds vinyl record crackle and noise for that authentic feel
  - reverb:      Adds space and atmosphere to create depth
  - lowpass:     Reduces high frequencies for that warm, mellow sound
  - tapeflutter: Adds wow/flutter pitch drift and tape hiss for vintage vibes

Examples:
  effect vinyl              Enable vinyl effect
  effect reverb             Enable reverb effect
  effect lowpass --enable=false  Disable lowpass filter
  effect tapeflutter        Enable tape flutter effect

arguments.name: effect-name
arguments.description: Name of the effect to manage

commands.name: volume
commands.description: Adjusts master volume

commands.name: start
commands.description:
Starts the LofiBeats service if it's not already running.

This command is useful when you want to:
1. Start the service explicitly before using other commands
2. Restart the service after a shutdown
3. Ensure the service is running without executing any playback commands

commands.name: shutdown
commands.description: Shuts down the LofiBeats service

commands.name: version
commands.description: Display version information

commands.name: interactive
commands.description: Enters an interactive mode for real-time control

commands.name: schedule
commands.description:
List or cancel scheduled playback actions.

Subcommands:
  list     List all scheduled actions
  cancel   Cancel a scheduled action by ID

Examples:
  schedule list              Show all scheduled actions
  schedule cancel <id>       Cancel a specific scheduled action

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all scheduled actions

commands.name: cancel
commands.description: Cancel a scheduled action

arguments.name: action-id
arguments.description: The ID of the action to cancel
